summary: The automated 'Fixer - Performance' agentic workflow (runs/29462636926) failed twice because `dotnet build/restore/run/cake` and network egress are blocked by the sandbox security policy, preventing the skill from proving optimizations with BenchmarkDotNet and running equivalence tests.

stepsToReproduce: Trigger the 'Fixer - Performance' workflow (scheduled or manual)
The agent attempts to run `dotnet build` or `dotnet test` to prove benchmark results
The sandbox security policy blocks all `dotnet` execution and network egress
The workflow reports missing tools and exits with failure

repoLinks.description: Failed workflow run (second attempt, from comment)
PR being worked on by the performance-fixer

analysis.summary: The performance-fixer agentic workflow is structurally incompatible with its sandbox environment: the skill requires `dotnet build`, `dotnet test`, and BenchmarkDotNet execution to satisfy its mandatory two-proof gate (speed + correctness), but the security sandbox blocks all dotnet CLI execution and network egress needed for NuGet restore. This has now failed twice on consecutive runs.

codeInvestigation.finding: The workflow runs on a schedule every 12h in the 'gh-aw-agents' environment. The performance-fixer skill requires BenchmarkDotNet measurements and equivalence tests via `dotnet build/test` — these are its mandatory two-proof gate before opening a PR.

Golden rule #2 mandates two proofs (speed AND correctness): a BenchmarkDotNet benchmark showing meaningful speedup plus an equivalence test proving bit-exact results. Both require running dotnet — skipping them is explicitly non-negotiable.

keySignals.interpretation: The sandbox firewall blocks all dotnet CLI invocations and outbound network needed for NuGet restore — this is not a transient error.

keySignals.text: Agent job 29499822018 failed. Missing Tools: dotnet (cake/build/run/test): security
keySignals.interpretation: Same failure on a retry run — confirms this is a persistent environment constraint, not a transient issue.

keySignals.text: Provide a pre-bootstrapped environment (natives in output/native + restored NuGet cache) or allow-list dotnet build/test and the natives/nuget endpoints
keySignals.interpretation: Two remediation paths identified by the agent itself: pre-bootstrapped environment or security policy relaxation for specific dotnet operations.

analysis.rationale: This is a build/CI infrastructure bug: the performance-fixer workflow is deployed in an environment that cannot satisfy the tool's mandatory requirements. The skill design requires dotnet execution and network access; the security sandbox prohibits both. The workflow will continue to fail on every scheduled run until the environment is changed or the skill is redesigned.
nextQuestions: Can the 'gh-aw-agents' environment be configured with dotnet SDK pre-installed and NuGet packages pre-restored (bootstrapped environment)?
Is it possible to allowlist specific dotnet endpoints (nuget.org, dotnet download URLs) in the sandbox firewall?
What did PR #3645 contain — was any code change produced before the proof phase failed?
Should the workflow be suspended until the environment issue is resolved to avoid repeated failure issues?

resolution.hypothesis: The gh-aw-agents sandbox blocks dotnet execution by security policy; the performance-fixer skill requires it. The fix is to either (a) pre-bootstrap the environment with dotnet + NuGet cache, or (b) allowlist the necessary dotnet operations in the security policy.

proposals.title: Pre-bootstrap the runner environment
proposals.description: Configure the 'gh-aw-agents' environment to include a pre-restored NuGet cache and pre-built native outputs so the agent can run `dotnet test` and BenchmarkDotNet without network egress.

proposals.title: Allowlist dotnet build/test in sandbox policy
proposals.description: Update the sandbox security policy to allow dotnet CLI operations and the nuget.org / dotnet download endpoints needed by the performance-fixer skill.

proposals.title: Temporarily disable the scheduled workflow
proposals.description: Set `report-failure-as-issue: false` in the workflow frontmatter or pause the schedule until the environment issue is resolved, to stop generating noise issues.

resolution.recommendedReason: Pre-bootstrapping keeps the security boundary intact while unblocking the dotnet proof steps. The workflow already documents this as the preferred alternative.

actionability.reason: Environment configuration change needed. Root cause is clear (sandbox blocks dotnet) but the fix requires infrastructure-level changes outside the SkiaSharp codebase.

actions.description: Apply build area and bug type labels
Post analysis explaining the root cause and proposed remediation paths
actions.comment:
## Triage Analysis

The `Fixer - Performance` workflow has failed twice with the same root cause: the sandbox security policy blocks all `dotnet` CLI operations and network egress, which the skill requires to satisfy its mandatory two-proof gate (BenchmarkDotNet speed measurement + equivalence test).

**Root cause:** The `performance-fixer` skill golden rule #2 mandates two proofs before opening a PR — a benchmark showing speedup and an equivalence test proving bit-exact results. Both require `dotnet build/test`, which the `gh-aw-agents` sandbox disallows by security policy.

**Remediation options:**
1. **Pre-bootstrap the environment** — populate `output/native/` with pre-built natives and pre-restore the NuGet cache so the agent can run `dotnet test` / BenchmarkDotNet without network egress. This is the agent's own suggested alternative.
2. **Allowlist specific dotnet operations** — update the sandbox security policy to permit `dotnet build/test` and the relevant NuGet/dotnet download endpoints.
3. **Short-term workaround** — set `report-failure-as-issue: false` in the workflow frontmatter to suppress repeated failure issues until the environment is fixed.

PR [#3645](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/3645) was being worked on when the failure occurred — worth checking if any code change was produced before the proof phase failed.
